title: Unusual sign-in becomes an incident candidate
summary: A customer-free identity event moves through the K-SNS security workflow without claiming live telemetry, dispatch, containment, or verification.

assessment.reason: Correlated identity anomalies exceed the synthetic review threshold.

recommendation.action: Revoke active sessions and require re-authentication

verification.expected: Existing sessions invalidated and next sign-in challenges successfully

residual_risk.reason: The identity remains exposed until an approved response is executed and independently verified.
residual_risk.next_step: Approve or reject the recommendation, then wait for execution and verification evidence.

capability_states.label: Portal and lifecycle presentation
capability_states.detail: Rendered inside the existing authenticated K-SNS UI.

capability_states.label: Scenario and evidence values
capability_states.detail: Deterministic, customer-free fixture for founder review.

capability_states.label: Private K-SNS API contract
capability_states.detail: Contract shape only; this synthetic walkthrough does not call a live API. The same-origin BFF and private backend boundary remain unchanged.

capability_states.label: Live KAI/KIF/KES/KEA participation
capability_states.detail: No live cross-product evidence is represented.

capability_states.label: Live action and verification
capability_states.detail: Requires approved execution and evidence from the owning response product.
